description_long:
Relaymark automates the lifecycle messaging that e-commerce brands otherwise assemble by hand: welcome series, abandoned-cart recovery, win-back campaigns, and post-purchase flows across email and SMS. It connects to Shopify and BigCommerce, builds a profile of every shopper from orders and browsing events, and runs journeys from a visual builder.

Two measurement choices distinguish the product from a generic campaign tool. Send-time optimization picks the hour each shopper is most likely to engage, and a holdout system measures true incremental revenue per flow rather than last-click attribution — a direct shot at the inflated numbers this category is known for.

The templates are the accumulated playbook: over 900 brands run on Relaymark, journey templates encode what works — the site cites a 4-message abandoned-cart series as its most-copied template — and every flow can A/B test subject lines, timing, and offers. Deliverability is treated as product surface too, with dedicated sending infrastructure, list hygiene tooling, and automatic suppression of unengaged addresses.

The edges of the product round out the pitch. Beyond Shopify and BigCommerce, Relaymark syncs audiences to Meta and Google ad platforms for suppression and lookalikes, webhooks stream every message event to the warehouse, and review-request flows integrate with Okendo and Judge.me. Onboarding includes migration help — the team rebuilds existing flows, typically inside two weeks, and a deliverability specialist reviews the first month of sends. That migration muscle matters in a category where switching costs are mostly accumulated flows: a store's welcome series, win-back campaigns, and post-purchase logic move with the vendor's help rather than being rebuilt by hand.

Pricing scales with contactable profiles, with email and SMS metered separately and a free tier for stores under 500 profiles. The company is 30 people, founded in 2020 and fully remote, with customers concentrated in fashion, beauty, and food & beverage.
